report_name: Qilin Ransomware Group Claims Attack on U.S. Contractor Jayeff Construction

threat_actors.name: Qilin
threat_actors.description: A prolific Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) group that claimed responsibility for the attack on Jayeff Construction. The group is known for its double-extortion tactics and providing sophisticated, customizable ransomware payloads to its affiliates.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Used by Qilin affiliates to exfiltrate sensitive data from victims like Jayeff Construction before encrypting files, as part of their double-extortion modus operandi.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Qilin ransomware payload was used to encrypt files on Jayeff Construction's network, a core component of the ransomware attack.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The Qilin ransomware payload actively seeks to disable or delete backups and shadow copies to prevent victims from easily recovering their data.

malware.name: Qilin
malware.description: A sophisticated, customizable ransomware payload written in Go and Rust, enabling cross-platform attacks on Windows, Linux, and VMware ESXi servers. It is used in double-extortion attacks.

identities.name: Jayeff Construction
identities.description: A U.S.-based general contractor specializing in commercial and retail construction that was listed as a victim on the Qilin ransomware group's data leak site.

infrastructure.name: Qilin Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: The official data leak site where the Qilin ransomware group publicly lists its victims and threatens to leak their exfiltrated data. Jayeff Construction was listed on this site around May 1, 2026.
